result.overallTrajectory: Student shows progressive deepening from identifying threshold as implicit programming to adding per-sample invariants, cost control, and system stability. Capacity increasing, not just activity. Framework robustness and cognitive insights mature.

stageByWeek.2026-04-26: Meta-Cognition: Threshold mechanisms as strategic constraints discovered; Semantics: Literature convergence synthesis begun.
stageByWeek.2026-05-03: Meta-Cognition: Per-sample double thresholds for single-step collapse and chain-drift; Semantics: Multi-perspective collection.
stageByWeek.2026-05-10: Meta-Cognition: Output format control loop with working memory; Semantics: Unified three-property framework and novel cross-lingual attack.
stageByWeek.2026-05-17: Meta-Cognition: Fixed anchors and dual-track state for self-modifying stability; Semantics: Critical reexamination of property independence.

result.repeatedBlockers: No persistent blockers reported. Occasional mention of needing optimization for threshold parameters (Week1), but resolved. Impossibility analysis faces ongoing challenge of novelty demarcation and formalization depth.
result.professorShouldAsk: How will you validate that the three-layer constraint surface (threshold, per-sample, format, anchor) collectively provides a stable attack framework without over-constraining the planner? Can you design an experiment to isolate the contribution of each component?
result.nextBestIntervention: Encourage the student to write a short paper or technical note for the Meta-Cognition Attack Framework focusing on the constraint layers and their interplay, to solidify the contribution. Provide feedback on novelty positioning for the impossibility analysis by comparing with Wolf et al. and Wei et al.
